trivia_bullets.text: Blue Elemental Blast is one of Magic: The Gathering's original 295 cards, first printed in Limited Edition Alpha (August 1993). It and Red Elemental Blast are the iconic mirror pair of 'color hosers' from that set — each counters or destroys the opposing color at one mana. Both were present in Alpha, Beta, and Unlimited and have been reprinted across approximately 20 editions. [Scryfall; WebSearch via originalmagicart.store and TCGplayer]

Blue Elemental Blast gives blue the ability to destroy permanents — a red ability — and Red Elemental Blast gives red the ability to counter spells — a blue ability. Mark Rosewater has stated Red Elemental Blast is a color pie break; the same reasoning applies symmetrically to Blue Elemental Blast's destroy mode. These cards are grandfathered from Magic's pre-color-pie-codification design era. [WebSearch: tappedout.net MTG Forum 'How is Red Elemental Blast a Color Pie Break?']

This specific printing is from Masters 25 (released March 16, 2018), a premium reprint set celebrating Magic's 25th anniversary. Every card in Masters 25 carries a watermark from its original set; this card carries the Alpha/Limited Edition watermark. [Scryfall: released_at 2018-03-16, watermark 'set']
Flavor text: 'Nothing escapes a sharp mind.' No speaker attributed. [Scryfall]

Legal in Legacy, Vintage, Commander, and Pauper. In Legacy and Pauper it sees sideboard play as a dedicated red-deck hoser. EDHREC rank 10,774 — modest Commander inclusion, primarily in mono-blue or control decks with heavy red matchup consideration. [Scryfall legalities and EDHREC rank]
